rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-aminofunctionalization (including diamination) of terminal alkenes via anodically generated thianthrenium-based dicationic adducts that undergo regiospecific single substitution by phthalimide and subsequent functionalization with a second nucleophile
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thianthrene (TT) generates a thianthrenium radical cation that adds to the alkene to form a dicationic bis-sulfonium adduct; the electrochemistry is upstream of the productive C-N bond-forming substitution steps

key_intermediates.name: alkenyl thianthrenium salt (33 / II) formed by E1cb-type elimination of dicationic adduct by phthalimide anion
β-phthalimido sulfur ylide intermediate (III) from phthalimide addition at β-position of alkenyl thianthrenium
allylic phthalimide byproduct (34, 3:1 Z/E) formed under proton-deficient conditions

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes thianthrene to its radical cation, which adds across the alkene to give a dicationic bis-sulfonium adduct; phthalimide anion then deprotonates/eliminates this adduct to an alkenyl thianthrenium salt, adds regiospecifically at the β-carbon to form a sulfur ylide, and is protonated by HNPhth (generated in the elimination) to deliver the β-phthalimido monosulfonium product, which is finally displaced by a second nucleophile.

probe_reagents.name: isolated alkenyl thianthrenium salt 33
probe_reagents.observation: Resubjection to KNPhth (4 equiv) + HNPhth (1 equiv) gave 1,2-phthalimidosulfonium 2 in 74% yield with no allylic byproduct 35; with KNPhth (5 equiv) only, 2 dropped to 15% and allylic byproduct 34 formed in 38% (3:1 Z:E), establishing 33 as on-pathway and showing proton balance controls selectivity

probe_reagents.name: time-course 1H NMR monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: After addition of KNPhth, dicationic adduct 32 is rapidly consumed to alkenyl thianthrenium 33, which is then converted to 2, consistent with sequential elimination then addition

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of 1,2-phthalimidosulfonium 2
probe_reagents.observation: Crystal structure confirms regiochemistry of internal phthalimide addition / external sulfonium

probe_reagents.name: ammonia surrogate screen (TBAN3, pyridine, Ph2C=NH, KNPhth)
probe_reagents.observation: Only KNPhth gives high yield (~80%) of internal addition product A; other nucleophiles favor terminal isomer B or eliminated/allylic products, highlighting KNPhth's unique regioselectivity

notes: Authors explicitly contrast with the alternative direct SN2-like substitution of the dicationic adduct, which would not explain the high internal selectivity; instead, an elimination/re-addition pathway through alkenyl thianthrenium 33 is favored because alkenyl sulfonium salts are more electrophilic at the β-position. Proton balance (HNPhth generated in the elimination step) is essential — its absence diverts the reaction to allylic substitution product 34, indicating a competing allylation pathway analogous to reported alkenyl thianthrenium allylation chemistry.
